name: Flix
summary: Functional, logic, and imperative language with a type and effect system featuring primitive effects, algebraic effects, heap effects, effect polymorphism, sub-effecting, effect exclusion, purity reflection, and associated effects. Combines effect rows with explicit subtyping over effect sets.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Flix describes a type and effect system with primitive effects, algebraic effects, heap effects, effect polymorphism, sub-effecting, effect exclusion, purity reflection, and associated effects.

The distinguishing feature versus Koka is **explicit sub-effecting**: a function performing only `<exn>` can be passed where a `<exn,async>` function is expected, and the type system makes the subtype lattice over effect sets a first-class concept rather than relying on row unification alone.

sections.title: Effect features in production
